headline: Mexican Drama 'Six Months' Lands French Distribution Ahead of Cannes Premiere
lede: Bruno Santamaría Razo's provocative coming-of-age narrative secures critical momentum with Epicentre Films acquisition before Critics' Week debut.
body:
Mexican independent cinema continues its global ascendance as Bruno Santamaría Razo's latest feature finds international traction. The filmmaker's sophomore project, *Six Months in a Pink and Blue Building*, has locked French distribution rights with Epicentre Films ahead of its prestigious Cannes Critics' Week world premiere.

Set against Mexico City's complex early 1990s landscape, the film promises an intimate exploration of youth and identity. Luxbox, the Paris-based sales agency, brokered the international distribution deal that positions the film for potential European art house circuit penetration.

Cinema observers note the growing international appetite for nuanced Latin American storytelling. Santamaría Razo's narrative approach—which reportedly blends documentary-like observation with narrative intimacy—represents a compelling new wave of Mexican filmmaking emerging on the global festival circuit.

The Critics' Week selection traditionally highlights emerging directorial voices, suggesting significant potential for *Six Months* to generate critical conversation beyond its initial festival run.
key_facts: World premiere at Cannes Critics' Week
Sophomore feature from Bruno Santamaría Razo
